_doc:
Chapter 1 evidence cards. Per PROPOSAL_player_driven_argument.md §3 (decoy revision 2026-05-16). Each entry is a piece of evidence the player can surface during Beat 4 (binder) through Beat 9 (archive research). 'Surfacing' means reading the corresponding evidence card via dialogue (Crab/Murrow/Whimsy/Halina lines), the v0/v2 binder UI, or item pickup. Surfaced evidence is consumed by (a) the argument-frame proposal in crab.json's synthesis options block, (b) the binder UI's evidence panel, (c) the future court-round controller during Phase 1 Present actions.

Version 2 (2026-05-16) — decoy revision additions:
  - New schema field: points_to_frames: Dictionary<frame_id, float> — weighted mapping showing which argument frames this evidence supports and by how much. Weights in [0.0, 1.0]; multiple frames can be supported by one card; weights need NOT sum to 1.0.
  - New schema field: source_npc — which NPC primarily surfaces this evidence in conversation. Used by Asia hint surface for 'go talk to Mr. Crab' nudges and by binder UI marginalia attribution.
  - Five new evidence entries: payment_receipts_sikorska, notice_timeline_april, property_transfer_2018, sikorska_age_visible, tenancy_act_notice_window_citation. These support the new decoy frames (substantive_defense / notice_period_failure / standing_wrong_party / incapacity_defense). Three carry NEW sets_flag values that require chapter1 flag declarations (deferred to next SAVE_VERSION bump per the proposal — dialogue triggers reference these via missing-key-fails-trigger semantics in the interim).

Two-pass authoring per AGENTS.md §Casebook authoring.
  Code owns: id, source, source_npc, discovered_when, argument_tags, context_tags, sets_flag, points_to_frames.
  Design owns: display_name, summary, press_lines, present_lines.

Schema:
  evidence: { <evidence_id>: {
    display_name (Design — short noun phrase),
    summary (Design — one-line description),
    source (Code — where the card surfaces: 'binder_page_<n>' / 'archive_<doc_id>' / 'witness_<npc_id>' / 'item_<item_id>'),
    source_npc (Code — primary NPC who surfaces this card in conversation: 'crab' / 'murrow' / 'whimsy' / 'halina' / 'asia' / 'cula' / 'none'),
    discovered_when (Code — a dialogue-runner trigger predicate; when true, the card becomes surfaceable),
    sets_flag (Code — chapter1 flag flipped true on surfacing; the binder_read_*, surfaced_*, and bonus-evidence flags are the v17+ set),
    argument_tags (Code — principle_tags from data/tag_taxonomy.json),
    context_tags (Code — context_tags from data/tag_taxonomy.json),
    points_to_frames (Code — Dictionary<frame_id_from_argument_frames_ch1.json, float in [0,1]>; weighted support; need NOT sum to 1.0),
    press_lines (Design — what Cula sees/says when reading the card),
    present_lines (Design — what Cula says when presenting this card in court; one per court-round opening_statement it's relevant to)
  } }
_owner_code: id, source, source_npc, discovered_when, sets_flag, argument_tags, context_tags, points_to_frames
_revision_note: Schema version 2 — decoy revision per PROPOSAL_player_driven_argument.md §3 (2026-05-16). Existing v1 entries gain source_npc + points_to_frames. Five new entries support the four decoy frames + optional incapacity. The five new surfaced_* flags referenced via sets_flag are NOT yet declared in state.gd reset_state() — they're deferred to the next SAVE_VERSION bump per the proposal; dialogue triggers reference them via missing-key-fails-trigger semantics until declared.

envelope_address_number_seven._status: Code-pass complete; Design text pending. v2: added source_npc + points_to_frames. The envelope strongly supports defective_service (the central locational defect) and weakly hints at notice_period_failure (because the timing dates are also on the envelope).

renewal_2019_number_twelve._status: Code-pass complete; Design text pending. v2: added source_npc + points_to_frames. The renewal page (countersigned by the landlord in 2019, address: number twelve) is the smoking gun for defective_service — proves landlord knowledge of the correct address. Weakly hints at standing_wrong_party because the renewal predates the 2018 transfer mentioned in property_transfer_2018 (some players may try to weave the two together).

renumbering_2015_fact._status: Code-pass complete; Design text pending. v2: added source_npc + points_to_frames. The renumbering is not a document; it's a building-history fact. Surfaceable via Crab's stairwell observation OR by reading the binder cross-reference. Pure defective-service evidence.

wojcik_witness_statement._status: Hydrated from data/items.json. Surfaced by halina.json client_meeting_r0_response_high / client_meeting_r1_response_high. Bonus evidence — sympathetic-stance carrier. v2: added points_to_frames. Mrs. Wójcik's statement (continuous-resident testimony) supports defective_service (the witness can testify Halina has lived at the same flat, defeating any landlord 'maybe she moved' counter) and weakly supports substantive_defense (the long tenancy is part of the merits picture).

return_to_sender_slip._status: Hydrated from data/items.json. Surfaced by halina.json client_meeting_r0_response_blunt / client_meeting_r1_response_high. Bonus evidence — blunt-procedural-stance carrier; also volunteered on high-trust r1 from any stance. v2: added points_to_frames. The bounced October 2018 envelope is the second smoking gun for defective_service (prior knowledge of the correct address). Pure defective-service evidence; the timing isn't relevant to notice_period_failure (the envelope was a rent statement, not an eviction notice).

lease_1962_inheritance_1987._status: Hydrated from data/items.json. Surfaced by halina.json client_meeting_r0_response_technical. Bonus evidence — technical-stance carrier. v2: added points_to_frames. The 1962/1987 lease chain supports substantive_defense (long unbroken tenancy is the merits picture) and weakly supports defective_service (the documented chain shows the correct address has been Halina's since 1987). Per the proposal §1, this evidence pointer is now load-bearing on the substantive-defense decoy.

landlord_contact._status: Hydrated from halina.json client_meeting_r2_response_high. Bonus evidence — high-trust reveal. Surfacing also flips chapter1.landlord_tip_received; seeds Ch4 landlord-intimidation thread. v2: added points_to_frames. The March personal visit ('think carefully about your situation') strengthens defective_service by establishing landlord knowledge AND a pattern of contact — the landlord clearly knew where Halina lived and chose to misaddress the formal notice deliberately.

rights_memo_article_6._status: Hydrated from data/items.json (rights_memo). The fair-hearing companion to the procedural binder; surfaced via Whimsy at Café Paragraf. v2: added points_to_frames. The Rights Memo supports defective_service indirectly via the Article-6 / fair-hearing Phase 2 sub-citation (no longer a top-level frame — see argument_frames_ch1.json defective_service_135bis.phase_2_sub_citations).

payment_receipts_sikorska._status: NEW in v2 — decoy revision. Anchor evidence for substantive_defense. Halina's records of six months of rent settlement (receipts kept in case the landlord 'forgets'). Surfaced during the client meeting on the sympathetic OR technical stance (when Halina produces her folder); also surfaceable via Crab's offhand mention during pre-binder dialogue ('she has paperwork — receipts, the kind you keep when you expect the landlord to forget'). The sets_flag chapter1.surfaced_payment_receipts is NOT yet declared in state.gd reset_state(); see _revision_note above. discovered_when uses Dictionary.get() semantics — when the flag is later declared, the predicate will resolve cleanly.

notice_timeline_april._status: NEW in v2 — decoy revision. Anchor evidence for notice_period_failure. The notice timing math: April 8 service to old number 7, April 28 actual receipt by Halina, hearing already run ex parte before April 28. The dates DO look short under the Tenancy Act notice windows — colorable enough to make this a credible decoy. Crab surfaces this implicitly when reading the binder envelope aloud; Murrow surfaces it explicitly with the Tenancy Act citation pointer in murrow_first_meeting. The sets_flag chapter1.surfaced_notice_timeline is deferred per _revision_note.

tenancy_act_notice_window_citation._status: NEW in v2 — decoy revision. Doctrinal pointer for notice_period_failure. Murrow surfaces the Tenancy Act notice-window citation in his first_meeting (the doctrinal pointer that completes the notice-period frame). Without this, notice_timeline_april alone is colorable but undirected; with both, the player has a real notice-period argument to commit to. The sets_flag chapter1.surfaced_tenancy_act_window is deferred per _revision_note.

property_transfer_2018._status: NEW in v2 — decoy revision. Anchor evidence for standing_wrong_party. Whimsy's civic-records hobby produces a property-transfer record showing the building changed hands in 2018; the landlord-of-record on the eviction notice is the prior owner. The theory IS colorable (junior counsel sometimes tries it) but wrong because the lease assigned forward at transfer — Sikorska's obligations and the new owner's rights run together. Whimsy surfaces this offhand during recruitment ('I keep a folder of who-owns-what. Marginal pastime. Occasionally useful'). The sets_flag chapter1.surfaced_property_transfer is deferred per _revision_note.

sikorska_age_visible._status: NEW in v2 — decoy revision. Anchor evidence for incapacity_defense (the optional hard-wrong frame). Sikorska's age is visible in the client meeting and documented in her file. The 'evidence' is Cula's projection — no NPC surfaces this AS a frame pointer; it's the player choosing to read Sikorska's age as procedurally significant. Game-punished per argument_frames_ch1.json incapacity_defense entry. The sets_flag chapter1.surfaced_sikorska_age is deferred per _revision_note.
